obligations.obligation:
The equipment/ product must successfully pass the relevant tests (as
specified in the applicable standards), in an accredited laboratory such as the one accredited by
National Accreditation Board for Testing and Calibration Laboratories (NABL) before the
commercialization of the product/ equipment/m...
obligations.due_date: within N days after production completion or within X months from approval date, whichever is earlier
obligations.short_summary: Ensure product passes relevant tests in NABL-accredited lab before commercialization.

obligations.obligation:
Type test is a design and quality validation
test, and the purchaser must satisfy itself by following an agreed quality assurance plan and special
tests, if any, agreed between the buyer and the supplier/manufacturer.
obligations.short_summary: Ensure product meets agreed specifications through quality assurance plan and special tests.

obligations.obligation:
The utility shall not reject the product/ equipment for supply against the contract on technical ground,
if the subject product/ equipment has successfully passed the type test as per relevant Standards.
obligations.short_summary: Product acceptance conditional on passing type test

obligations.obligation:
Broad Guidelines
a) The type tests on equipment, for which testing facility is available in India, shall preferably be
conducted in India in any independent laboratory approved by the Government or the laboratories
accredited by the National Accreditation Body of the Country.
obligations.due_date: within six months from procurement date
obligations.short_summary: Mandatory testing of equipment in India to ensure compliance with national standards.

obligations.obligation:
b) The type tests on indigenous equipment, for which testing facility is not available in India, shall be
conducted in a laboratory of a foreign Country accredited by the National Accreditation Body of that
Country.
obligations.short_summary: Test indigenous equipment abroad due to lack of local facilities

obligations.obligation:
c) The type tests on imported equipment shall be conducted in an Indian laboratory or foreign
laboratory accredited by the National Accreditation Body of the Country where the type test has
been conducted.
obligations.short_summary: Mandatory type testing of imported equipment in accredited laboratories.

obligations.obligation:
Guidelines for the Type Tests for Major Equipment in Power Sector Page| 3/16d) The type tests conducted in-house by a manufacturer shall also be acceptable provided the
laboratory is accredited by the National Accreditation Body of the Country and the test has been
conducted in the presence of a rep...
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
Such type test report shall record the details of such witness
including the signature/ authentication in the type test report.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
e) The result of all type tests shall be recorded properly in the Type Test Reports (TTRs) containing
sufficient information like ratings, relevant drawings, model number, test circuit, calculations
(if any), photographs and compliance to the relevant standards.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
f) The date of the type test report shall be reckoned from the date of issuance of the complete type
test report.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
The type test report should be issued by the laboratory within 15 days from the date of
completion of the last test of the series of tests under the type test of that equipment as per the
relevant standards.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
In case type test report including the provisional ‘type test’ report is not issued
within 15 days, type test report shall be reckoned from the 15th day of completion of the last test of
the series of test under the type test.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
The periodicity of type test
conducted on the equipment i.e., the period for which Type Test Reports (TTRs) shall be acceptable
by the user/ utility, and shall be as per given Annexures [Annexure-I (Transmission), Annexure-II
(Distribution), Annexure-III (Thermal), Annexure-IV (Hydro)].
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
h) For the purpose of deciding the acceptability of the type test reports, the date of issue of the type
test report shall be reckoned with reference to the date of the submission of the bid by the bidder.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
Guidelines for the Type Tests for Major Equipment in Power Sector Page| 4/16That is the type test report in respect of the concerned equipment shall not be older than the period
specified in these Guidelines as on the date of submission of the bid by the bidder.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
i) Type test report of 220 kV voltage class equipment shall be valid for 230 kV voltage class equipment
and vice versa as the highest system voltage is same in both the cases provided the Basic Insulation
Level (BIL) is same.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
j) All the testing equipment used for type testing shall be duly calibrated and the valid calibration
reports shall form part of the Type Test Reports.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
k) The equipment shall be supplied from the same manufacturing works, where from the sample unit
was manufactured and successfully type tested as per relevant standard or at the works of Parent
organization in case of technology transfer/ Joint Venture (JV) for the initial period of 03 years from
th...
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
In case of own manufacturing plant at different location within India, the type test of the original
manufacturing works shall also be acceptable for the equipment manufactured and supplied from
the different location subject to the following conditions:
(i) the relevant standard does not bar the sa...
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
In case failure(s) of the equipment manufactured & supplied from that location is reported to the
Authority, the Authority shall direct the concerned manufacturer to conduct Type Test for that
equipment manufactured & supplied from that location.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
Till the time Type Test is conducted
successfully, the manufacturer shall not manufacture & supply such equipment from that location.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
m) The type tests shall be repeated if there is change in technology or basic design or material or
manufacturing process or combination of any of the above or the type testing criteria in the
respective Standards have undergone a change/ amendment/ revision.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
The
OEM/ manufacturer shall submit an undertaking to this effect.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
However, fresh type testing shall be limited to only those tests for which test procedure/
method, any technical requirement or parameters to be tested have been changed in the revised
Standards.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
In such cases, enough time as mutually agreed between the user and manufacturer, shall
be given to manufacturer to comply with the revised provision in the Standards.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
o) The utilities shall co-relate the need of repeating type test(s) to changes in design/ technology and
may emphasize/ insist for stage inspection/quality check and hold points to ensure quality of the
component/ material used in the manufacturing of the equipment.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
p) The change in the make of component(s) of the equipment shall not be the criteria for repetition of
type tests for the equipment provided that the component of new make has been successfully type
tested and its use shall not affect the functionality, performance and reliability of the equipment.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation: The type test reports of the component of new make shall be submitted.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
However, the utilities should
refrain from making it as a regular practice.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
The utility shall clearly specify which type tests shall be
repeated in the event of award of the contract and quotation for such tests shall be invited
separately in the price bid.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

January 2026Guidelines for the Type Tests for Major Equipment in Power Sector
1. Background
Prior to commercialization of the equipment/ product, the equipment/ product passes through the
product development stages. The equipment/ product must successfully pass the relevant tests (as
specified in the applicable standards), in an accredited laboratory such as the one accredited by
National Accreditation Board for Testing and Calibration Laboratories (NABL) before the
commercialization of the product/ equipment/material. One of such tests is a “type test” which is
generally performed on any ...
